note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/Bochner/Basic.lean:156 `irreducible_def integral {_ : MeasurableSpace α} (μ : Measure α) (f : α → G) : G` is the general Bochner integral w.r.t. any measure; specialized to volume on Fin n → ℝ / product measures it IS the classical multiple integral, and Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/Pi.lean:79 `integral_fin_nat_prod_eq_prod` (n-variable Fubini) reduces it to iterated single integrals as in the textbook definition. No dedicated 'multipleIntegral' decl exists, so this is a strict generalization, not an exact match.
